title: Stend VI
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{CelestialBody |image=[[File:StendVI.png]] |name=Stend VI |region=[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Stend system/Legends|Stend system]] |sector=[[Juris sector/Legends|Juris sector]] |system=[[Stend system/Legends|Stend system]] |coordinates=P-17 |xyz= |distance= |suns= |climate=[[Temperature/Legends|Temperate]] |gravity=[[Gravity/Legends|Standard]] |terrain=*[[Mountain/Legends|Mountains]] *Plains *[[City/Legends|Urban]] |water=Moderate [[Hydrosphere/Legends|hydrosphere]] |interest=*[[Imperial-class spaceport|Imperial-class starport]] *[[Kuda's Compound]] *[[The Pits (cantina)|The Pits]] *[[Raptor Run]] |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies=*[[Human/Legends|Humans]] *Other [[Species/Legends|species]] |socialgroup= |language= |government=[[Politics/Legends|Representative]] [[Democracy/Legends|democracy]] |population=2.2 billion |demonym= |cities=[[Queo City]]{{JournalCite|10|Galaxywide NewsNets}} |imports=[[Foodstuff/Legends|Foodstuffs]] |exports=Consumer goods |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] }} {{Quote|Despite the best effort of Mannd's forces, Stend VI remains a favorite neutral meeting place for some pirate groups.|A news report by Sektor Newsline 242|}} '''Stend VI''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located within the [[Stend system/Legends|Stend system]] of the [[Juris sector/Legends|Juris sector]]. Lying on the [[Five Veils Route]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]], it was a terrestrial world with a [[Temperature/Legends|temperate]] climate and a terrain dominated by [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]], plains, and [[City/Legends|urban]] areas. Stend VI's mostly [[Human/Legends|Human]] population was governed [[Democracy/Legends|democratically]], although the planet also served as a manufacturing site for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. At first glance an unordinary world of the Empire, Stend VI came to be plagued by continuous violence as several [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gangs]] rose to prominence on the planet. Among the first gangs, [[the Skulls]] gained an infamous reputation for [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorizing]] innocent people, while [[the Knights]] were dedicated to the principles of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and opposed the Skulls. The [[Stend VI Security Forces|local police]] were outmatched by the gangs, and even [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] intervention was ineffective at first, leading to the creation of the specialized [[Dark Riders]] unit of mounted [[Storm commando/Legends|Storm Commandos]]. A prominent location on Stend VI, the [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] known as [[The Pits (cantina)|The Pits]] was a vibrant establishment where arguments either flared up into brawls or were settled on the cantina's private [[Raptor Run]] racing course. The Pits' owner, famed racing champion [[Blizz Pinnix]], occasionally competed at Raptor Run. Although owned by Pinnix, The Pits was run by the [[Herglic/Legends|Herglic]] businessman [[Kaylo NaKuda]], who also owned the adjacent vehicle repair garage and dealership [[Kuda's Compound]], which was staffed by [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]]. During the Empire's reign, a group of individuals worked together with Pinnix, the Knights, and the [[Sector Ranger/Legends|Sector Ranger]] [[Narra Mesyne]] to bring about the Herglic [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord's]] downfall. ==Description== Stend VI was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located within the [[Stend system/Legends|Stend system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Juris sector/Legends|Juris sector]]. It occupied the sixth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position around its [[Star/Legends|star]] and lay on the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] known as the [[Five Veils Route]], which connected it to the [[Ninzam system/Legends|Ninzam]] and [[Lybeya system/Legends|Vergesso systems]]. Stend VI orbited its star every 230 local [[Standard day/Legends|days]], each of which was twenty-three [[Standard hour/Legends|standard hours]] long, and had three [[Moon/Legends|moons]], two of which loomed large in the planet's sky. The Outer Rim world had standard [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]], a breathable Type I [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], and a [[Temperature/Legends|temperate]] climate, with [[Winter/Legends|winters]] being relatively mild. The surface of Stend VI featured [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]], plains, [[City/Legends|urban]] areas, and a moderate [[Hydrosphere/Legends|hydrosphere]]. There were no [[Diet/Legends|predators]] that could pose a particular threat to the planet's populace. ==History== ===Origins of a war=== {{Quote|We must stop these…''villains''. We must stop them before someone is injured.|Finegan Flint, on the Skulls|''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''}} [[File:JengSeth.jpg|thumb|right|180px|The founder of the Skulls swoop gang, Jeng Seth]] During the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], several [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gangs]] arose on Stend VI, leading to daily skirmishes in the planet's skies. The group that was considered to be the first real gang was [[the Skulls]]. It was formed around the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]],''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'', which is set in the [[Time/Legends|time]] period between the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], states that [[the Skulls]] were formed "nearly six [[Standard year/Legends|years]]" ago. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates those two battles to the years [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], respectively, thus placing the formation of the Skulls in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] at the latest. when the seventeen-year-old [[Psychopathy|sociopath]] [[Jeng Seth]] grew bored of shaking down store owners for protection [[Currency/Legends|money]] and [[Thief/Legends|picking]] pockets. Discovering a love for speed and danger in the streets of the essentially lawless Stend VI, Seth gathered his street friends and formed the Skulls, which began to commit robberies and [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorize]] innocents. When the Skulls began to make a name for themselves in the local news by terrorizing the inhabitants of Stend VI, the twenty-year-old idealist and local [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] [[Finegan Flint]] started to gather his friends in order to stop the menace of Seth's gang. Fascinated by historical and fictional accounts of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knights]] of the [[Order 66/Legends|disbanded]] [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' Flint led his friends into confronting a member of the Skulls and telling him to surrender himself to the [[Stend VI Security Forces|local police]]. This started a feud between the two groups that eventually turned into a gang war. Flint's group, which came to be known as [[the Knights]], was dedicated to the ideals of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], but neither the local [[Planetary Security Forces|law enforcement agencies]] nor the subsequently deployed [[Imperial Military/Legends|forces of the Empire]] viewed it as anything more than yet another criminal organization. ===Legacy of the Skulls=== {{Quote|You said we needed money, so I got us the highest paying run out there.|Nuendo Rash, overheard in The Pits|''[[Hideouts & Strongholds]]''}} [[File:Swooperknights.jpg|thumb|left|225px|The Knights swoop gang, which fought against the Skulls and the Razers]] By 2 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], the Skulls had relocated to the [[Hook Nebula/Legends|Hook Nebula]]. A new swoop gang known as the [[Razers]] emerged on Stend VI and resumed the Skulls' campaign of terror, at the same [[Time/Legends|time]] specializing in stealing vehicles and parts. The Knights gang eventually refocused on combating the Razers. Maintaining only a handful of [[operative]]s on Stend VI in apprehension of inciting further conflict on the already violence-plagued world, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] tried to recruit members of the Knights at some point between the years [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'', which mentions that the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] is interested in recruiting [[the Knights]], is set in the [[Time/Legends|time]] period between the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates those two battles to the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], respectively, while establishing the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]] as the final event before the beginning of [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]. The Razers came to be hired by the local [[Herglic/Legends|Herglic]] businessman [[Kaylo NaKuda]], who purchased stolen merchandise from the gang only to sell it as supposedly legitimate goods at low prices. NaKuda's illegal activities, which also included [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]], eventually led the [[Sector Ranger/Legends|Sector Ranger]] [[Special Enforcement Officer/Legends|Special Enforcement Officer]] [[Narra Mesyne]] to try and discover enough evidence against the Herglic to bring him to justice for his crimes. To that end, Mesyne posed as a new [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] [[Swoop racing/Legends|racer]] called Miraj, who attempted to collect a prize for successfully completing the race course known as [[Raptor Run]]. The [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Scoundrel/Legends|scoundrel]] [[Cohden K'Reye]] visited Stend VI at some point during the Empire's reign. They spent time in the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] known as [[The Pits (cantina)|The Pits]] and later described the establishment in a [[Cohden K'Reye's treatise|treatise]] highlighting several recreation-related locations of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The Pits was also once visited by the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Nuendo Rash]], who was overheard telling a companion that they had both been hired to transport [[Medic/Legends|medical]] gear to [[Fortress Keremark]] on the [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony]] planet [[Risban]]. Rashh was arrested after making the comment. ===Troubles for Blizz Pinnix=== [[Blizz Pinnix]], the owner of The Pits, was bidding at one point for Stend VI to host a qualifying race for the [[Triple Crown]] competition of the [[swoopchasing]] discipline. The Triple Crown races were held on the planets of [[Najiba/Legends|Najiba]], [[Neftali/Legends|Neftali]], [[Redcap]], and [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]]. Up to that point, the qualifying races for the competition were traditionally held on [[Garos IV]], [[Omman]], and [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].''[[The Black Sands of Socorro]]'' [[File:IrlynResk.png|thumb|right|180px|Lieutenant Irlyn Resk and the Dark Riders]] At some point during the swoop gang warfare on Stend VI, the Empire responded to the local [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police's]] requests for assistance and sent in a [[Fighter wing/Legends|wing]] of [[64-Y Swift 3 Repulsorlift Sled|repulsorlift sleds]]. At first, the [[Hoversled/Legends|sleds]] appeared to be an effective solution, but eventually they were overwhelmed by the large numbers of the swoop bikers, and many of the Imperial vehicles were lost. As a result, the Imperial [[Commander/Legends|commanders]] on Stend VI formed the [[Dark Riders]], a specialized [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of elite [[Storm commando/Legends|Storm Commandos]] led by famed [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout trooper]] [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Irlyn Resk]] and mounted on heavily modified [[XR-10 Shadowhawk|speeder bikes]]. Resk secretly wanted to confiscate the [[Black Raptor]], Pinnix's infamous [[XP-2000 prototype speeder bike|swoop]], for himself. When Pinnix discovered the Black Raptor had mysteriously disappeared, the famous racer put out a very large reward for the swoop's return, which attracted to Stend VI many individuals eager to look for the lost vehicle as well as [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]]. There were many suspects in the disappearance, including members of the swoop gangs, NaKuda, and Lieutenant Resk. {{RPGseed}} Eventually, a group of individuals were successful in tracking down the swoop bike and received a large reward, although their fortune was subsequently threatened by the activities of Stend VI's swoop gangs. Around that time, NaKuda's use of slaves at [[Kuda's Compound]] was discovered by a group of individuals who were themselves captured and put to work in the Compound's garages. The group met the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] slave [[Sil Vaturha]] there. The individuals ultimately escaped and, after finding Pinnix, presented The Pits' owner with convincing evidence of NaKuda's criminal activities. With Pinnix's assistance as well as that of Miraj and the Knight swoop gang, the group defeated the Herglic [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] and his henchmen. {{Endgame}} ===Pirate activities=== {{Quote|They've gone somewhere else. Why? Beats me. But if they had packed it in and split up, some of them would have washed up here. The split-offs always do.|Grady Mannd, on the absence of any members of the Khuiumin Survivors on Stend VI|}} By the [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] [[2 ABY/Legends|37:6:8]], Stend VI had become a neutral meeting place favored by certain [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] groups. [[Defense Minister (Stend VI)|Defense Minister]] [[Grady Mannd]] of Stend VI unsuccessfully tried to use the local military forces to drive the pirates away from the planet. On that date, [[Sektor 242 NewsLine|Sektor Newsline 242]] broadcast a news report from [[Queo City]] on Stend VI. The report discussed the possible reasons for the inexplicably sudden decrease in the number of attacks by the [[Khuiumin Survivors]] pirate gang. It also quoted Mannd's belief that the Survivors had relocated to a different region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] to carry out their attacks, as evidenced by a lack of any reported members of the gang on Stend VI. ==Inhabitants== [[File:Skulls_Swooper.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The Skulls were one of the swoop gangs causing mayhem on Stend VI.]] Stend VI had a population of 2.2 billion, most of which were [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. The inhabitants of the Outer Rim world were [[Politics/Legends|governed]] by a representative [[Democracy/Legends|democracy]] allied with the [[Galaxy/Legends|Galactic]] Empire, in which the planet served the role of a manufacturing and processing site. The society on Stend VI had access to space-level technology and maintained an [[Imperial-class spaceport|Imperial-class starport]]. The planet exported consumer goods to other worlds while at the same time importing [[Foodstuff/Legends|foodstuffs]] from elsewhere. Considered to be a mostly typical Imperial world, at first glance Stend VI appeared to be peaceful and even dull. These qualities, however, eventually led to the planet becoming home to some of the galaxy's most infamous swoop gangs, with the gangs of Stend VI being especially problematic to the Empire. The gangs were responsible for extensive damage to property on the planet and significant [[Death/Legends|loss]] of life. The Stend VI Security Forces were determined to stop the swoop-mounted criminals, but they faced a severe shortage of trained personnel and equipment necessary to combat the growing threat of the gangs, with even Imperial [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsor]] sled patrols proving ineffective. ==Locations== ===The Pits=== {{Main|The Pits}} {{Quote|There are two distinct ways to enter The Pits:
1) Fly in (via the Hive)
2) Walk in (via the door)
The choice you make will have a definite effect on how you are viewed by the clientele—and management, too!
1) Deserving of at least a modicum of respect
2) Bantha droppings
Get the holo-picture? Good.|Cohden K'Reye|}} [[File:ThePitsStendVI.png|thumb|right|180px|Exterior of the Pits, with the Hive visible]] A prominent location on Stend VI was the swoop racer bar known as The Pits. Lying within an urban area of low buildings and [[Tree/Legends|trees]], The Pits had a ground floor with a street-level entrance as well as an underground level. An unusual feature of the cantina was its main entrance. Since The Pits was built with the motto of swoop enthusiasts in mind—"Why walk when you can fly?"—most swoop [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] entered the establishment through the [[Hive]], a tall, cylindrical tower on the cantina's roof. The Hive was honeycombed with hundreds of cubicles known as stalls, which allowed visitors of The Pits to dock their aerial vehicles. Docking in the stalls was regulated by the [[Hive Traffic Control]], a structure made almost entirely of [[Transparisteel/Legends|transparisteel]] and mounted on a thin support beam at the top of the large landing tower. The Hive also featured [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolifts]] leading to the ground level, which was set up as a [[Museum/Legends|museum]] dedicated to the history of repulsorlift vehicles and racing. The museum's collection also included the trophies of The Pits' owner, legendary swoop racer Blizz Pinnix. ====The cantina proper==== {{Quote|The Pits is aptly named. Not only is it a swoop racer bar, but it's dirty, dark, and sunk into the ground. If you can't tell a landspeeder from a Star Destroyer, this isn't your kind of place. The crowd is obnoxious, dangerous, and fearless. ''[…]'' Minor arguments can quickly escalate into massive brawls, especially between rival gangs. If you don't believe me, just take a quick count of all the blast marks in the wall!|Cohden K'Reye|}} [[File:ArgumentInThePits.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The Pits was a lively and colorful establishment.]] Another set of turbolifts led from the ground level to the underground floor, where the cantina proper was located. It was a lively and welcoming place full of both professional and amateur swoop and [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] bike pilots of several [[Species/Legends|species]], including members of swoop gangs. Due to always appearing in public wearing a signature racing [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]], no one knew what Pinnix looked like, so it was possible that the owner of The Pits was actually present in the cantina at any given moment without anyone recognizing the famous racer. The day-to-day operations of The Pits were overseen by the Herglic businessman Kaylo NaKuda, who kept secret from Pinnix his criminal activities. Since many of the bar's patrons were highly competitive in nature, disagreements were commonplace and were most often settled on the race course known as Raptor Run, although arguments occasionally erupted into brawls, which sometimes involved [[Blaster/Legends|blasterfire]]. Such conflicts rarely got out of hand due to the presence of the [[Reigat]] racer-turned-[[Bouncer (occupation)/Legends|bouncer]] [[Chugg]]. [[Droid/Legends|Droids]] were [[Anti-droid sentiment/Legends|not welcome]] in The Pits, and there were rumors that any mechanical beings who did find their way inside the establishment ended up in a massive storeroom of spare parts. ===Kuda's Compound=== {{Main|Kuda's Compound}} [[File:Kaylo_NaKuda.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Kaylo NaKuda in front of Kuda's Compound]] Kuda's Compound was a large, multistory building located next to The Pits. Owned by the Herglic Kaylo NaKuda, who also ran the adjacent cantina, the Compound offered the combined services of repulsorlift vehicle repairs and modifications, as well as dealership of new and used vehicles and parts. It was a profitable operation that offered competitive prices and full services of resident mechanics to its customers. When not fulfilling his duties as a bouncer at The Pits, Chugg, who was also NaKuda's [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]], could be encountered at the Compound. In truth, the commercial success of Kuda's Compound was due to NaKuda employing a pair of illegal methods. Firstly, almost all the vehicles and parts available at the Compound were stolen either by members of the local Razer swoop gang or imported from offworld by [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]]. The Herglic purchased these stolen goods at a low enough price to be able to offer the Compound's services at invitingly low costs to its clients. Secondly, the "mechanics" dealing with customers were just [[Actor/Legends|actors]], while the real work was done in hidden, underground garages by a dozen slaves who were outfitted with [[AA-2 Keeper|subdermal implants]] that tracked their movements and prevented escape. One of the slaves was Sil Vaturha, a Twi'lek [[Gender/Legends|female]] orphan who possibly had some of the greatest technological skills of the inhabitants of the Juris and neighboring [[Sector/Legends|sectors]]. ===Raptor Run=== {{Main|Raptor Run}} {{Quote|While most disagreements are settled on the Pits' race course, fights do have a tendency to flare up with a startling fury.|Cohden K'Reye|}} [[File:Blizz_Pinnix.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Blizz Pinnix, legendary swoop racer and the owner of The Pits]] A zone of hundreds of square [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] originally designated as an Imperial [[Ordinance Detonation Test Area]], Raptor Run was filled with rocky cliffs, canyons, and serpentine valleys, as well as labyrinthine, jagged paths twisting through them. The area also had fields of craters and mazes of slagged buildings as a result of former testing of Imperial munitions. Blizz Pinnix bought the land for a low price and turned it into The Pits' private race course. The eighty-five-kilometer-long course was rumored to have resulted in career-ending injuries, most of which were fatal, for more than a thousand swoop racers. A testament to Raptor Run's difficulty was that only three competitors had ever completed the course in under twenty [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]]—a [[Unidentified Tatooinian|young Tatooinian]], the stunt racer [[Lev Xestra]], and Pinnix himself, who held the record for the fastest run on the track. Pinnix occasionally entered the competitions held at Raptor Run, where he rode the Black Raptor, his renowned swoop and the namesake of the course. ==Behind the scenes== Stend VI was first mentioned in ''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'', a [[1993]] sourcebook written by [[Simon Smith]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Stend [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Stend VI, in grid square P-17. The [[1997]] sourcebook ''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'' provides several adventure seeds set on the planet for use with ''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'', and this article assumes as many of those scenarios as possible play out as described without contradicting each other. Alternate scenarios of how the player characters may interact with the character Narra Mesyne are also presented in the adventure seeds. One of these options suggests that the characters are originally sent to Stend VI to assist Mesyne in her investigation. Another option is for the player characters to be hired by the character Kaylo NaKuda to discover who is [[Spy/Legends|spying]] on him, resulting in the characters preventing Mesyne from succeeding at her mission or even capturing her. ==Appearances== *{{JournalCite|10|Galaxywide NewsNets}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Stend VI}} *''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'' *''[[The Black Sands of Socorro]]'' *''[[Hideouts & Strongholds]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Factory worlds]] [[Category:Galactic Empire planets]] [[Category:Planets in the Outer Rim Territories]] [[Category:Stend system locations]] [[Category:Terrestrial planets]] [[Category:Type I atmosphere planets]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Organizations of Stend VI]] |[[Category:Stend VI locations]] }}
